name: feature
description: Simplified member join and verify journey: minimal web UX, channel-branded bot DMs, fair instant-ban policy, admin More details with social profile scoring, blockchain profile as true north
goals: Frictionless member UX — verification feels routine; background checks invisible
Clear outcomes — approve DM with channel link; restrict shows Account restricted only
Fair instant ban — local evasion and configured severe categories only
Cross-channel accountability — prior bans surface in admin review with dated reasons
Compounding blockchain profile enriched on each join and evasion catch
Operator visibility — all outcomes in admin channel with More details and social API enrichment
nonGoals: Showing ban reasons or network history to joiners
Full OSINT analyst dashboard beyond Telegram admin cards
Requiring social links on the verify page in v1
Replacing the /security wizard (consumes its policy outputs only)
technicalNotes: Extend handlers.py, telegram_actions.py, tokens.py, matching.py, verify.py, static/verify.* — do not replace network-trust-registry stack. Split local ban evasion (BLOCK) from cross-channel on-chain history (PENDING). Add singulr/services/social_profile.py with pluggable provider; mock provider for tests. BanRegistry registrar ACL already exists from network-trust-registry pack.
_priority_order: 1. Token policy and member-visible copy (DM + web)
2. Matching decision tree — network history vs local evasion
4. Structured auto-ban and on-chain register on approve
_step_size: One logical change per commit. Quality over speed.

requirements.description: One active verification token per user — new join invalidates previous unused tokens
acceptance: create_token marks prior unused tokens for same telegram_user_id as used or deletes them
Only the latest token validates successfully
token_expiry_minutes remains 10 by default
pytest tests/test_tokens.py includes one-active-link test

test.description: Add test_tokens test: second create_token invalidates first unused token

implement.description: In create_token, invalidate or mark used all other unused tokens for telegram_user_id before issuing new token

verification: New join request yields only one valid verify URL
Old link returns link_expired on precheck/submit

requirements.description: Channel-branded join DM and approval DM with link to open the channel
acceptance: Join DM copy includes Finish joining {Channel Name} pattern without Singulr security jargon
Approval DM includes t.me/c/ or invite link to the channel
Pending DM uses PRD copy: verification under review; notification in Telegram DMs when complete
Deny/restrict DM does not include detailed ban reason
pytest covers join DM and approve DM content

requirements.description: Minimal verify webpage — generic restricted copy, under-review state, no security jargon

Pending shows under-review message distinct from restricted
Success state is minimal — no lock emoji or fraud-prevention copy
Page title and headings do not mention blockchain or fingerprinting

requirements.description: Matching policy: cross-channel on-chain/network history → PENDING; local ban evasion stays BLOCK
acceptance: chain.is_banned true without local evasion match returns PENDING not BLOCK
Network reputation score in review band returns PENDING not BLOCK
Local ban evasion above auto_deny threshold still returns BLOCK
pytest tests/test_matching.py covers cross-channel pending vs local evasion block

test.description: Add matching tests: mock chain.is_banned → PENDING; mock evasion → BLOCK

implement.description: Refactor check_known_bad: separate network_history_outcome from local_evasion; map network-only hits to PENDING

refactor.description: Document decision tree in matching module docstring

verification: API submit returns pending for on-chain-only hit
API submit returns block for high-confidence local evasion

get_effective_channel_policy exposes instant_ban_categories with defaults impersonation and bot_abuse
